items.rationale: Launched a new Columbia research center focused on blockchain infrastructure, funded by the Ethereum Foundation; his most emphatic recent enthusiasm.

citations.quote: New research center focused on blockchain infrastructure @CUSEAS ! Made possible by @ethereumfndn. Super-excited about this, more to come!
Brief video about the new center @cu_eth_research ... that we're using to accept donations and unlock additional matching funds from @ethereumfndn

items.rationale: Co-authored new foundational paper tackling verification that agreed-upon services/resources are actually being provided; an active research frontier for him.

citations.quote: developing some of the foundations for DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure) networks, and specifically the key problem of verifying that the agreed-upon services/resources are indeed being provided

items.rationale: Ongoing research on identifying which validators to hold accountable when a protocol stalls (liveness violations); core to his work and the new center.

citations.quote: When a blockchain protocol stalls (i.e., has a liveness violation), which validators should be held accountable? See this new paper

items.rationale: Optimistic that SNARKs and even more exotic cryptographic primitives can be made practical over the coming decades; recurring seminar theme.
items.age_note: Stated Dec 2024 (~12 months old); echoed by recurring zk/TEE/MPC seminars noted Oct 2025, but the explicit optimism is from late 2024.

citations.quote: OH (from a famous theoretical computer scientist): "if SNARKs can be made practical, *anything* can be made practical"
a prediction about future practical progress on advanced cryptographic primitives

items.rationale: Sustained teaching/research output on automated market makers, loss-versus-rebalancing, and crypto contest theory across mining, staking, execution tickets, shared sequencing.
items.age_note: Tutorials/talks from Dec 2024 (~12 months); topic reaffirmed as recurring seminar theme Oct 2025.

citations.quote: My EC talk on Tullock contests in crypto/web3 is now on YT ... Covers mining, staking, execution tickets, shared sequencing.
Videos from my LVR tutorial with @jason_of_cs @ciamac at EC '24 last summer are up!

items.rationale: Co-authored a recent primer on arcade tokens with a16z colleagues; aligns with his stated interest in digital property rights.

citations.quote: New primer on arcade tokens with my ever-amazing @a16zcrypto colleagues @skominers @eddylazzarin @milesjennings

items.rationale: His framework warns that restaking reuses validators across services, making security combinatorial; small shocks can cascade unless networks are sufficiently overcollateralized.
items.age_note: Core paper from Aug 2024 (~16 months old), with an accompanying talk Sep 2024; not restated since, treat as persisted.

citations.quote: New paper with @n_durvasula offers a framework to reason about the risks of restaking.
the maximum-possible length of a cascade of attacks is also governed by the overcollateralization factor

items.rationale: Emphasizes that attack cost must significantly exceed attack profit; flags fragility when the cost-profit buffer is thin and shocks occur.
items.age_note: From Aug 2024 restaking thread (~16 months old); not subsequently restated.

citations.quote: compare the cost incurred by attacking validators ... with the estimated profit of an attack. Ideally, the former is significantly bigger

risk_regime.rationale: Roughgarden's corpus is entirely research, education, and protocol-mechanism focused. He expresses no opinion on market direction, asset prices, or risk appetite, and names no tradeable tokens with a directional view.

notes: Subject is a research scientist (a16z crypto head of research, Columbia professor), not a market/portfolio voice — his 'excitement' and 'concern' are about research areas and technical risks, not investments. No specific tokens are discussed as positions (ETH appears only via Ethereum Foundation funding his center; 'arcade tokens' is treated as a sector/concept, not a tradeable token). Most-recent and highest-conviction signal is the Sept 2025 Columbia blockchain-infrastructure center (ETH-aligned). DePIN (Jun 2025) and consensus/accountability (May 2025) are the freshest research enthusiasms. Restaking risk is his clearest 'concern' but stems from Aug 2024 work and is marked persisted given no recent restatement. zk/SNARKs optimism and MEV/mechanism-design interest persist from late-2024 statements, partially corroborated by Oct 2025 seminar-topic lists. No reversals detected across the corpus.
